items.label: storefront-dev
messages: Now let me explore the existing codebase structure.
The directories already exist. Let me check their contents.
Files already exist. Let me read their current state and the reference patterns.
Good - all 4 files already exist and look well-implemented. Let me run the tests first.
All 26 tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server as required.
Dev server returned HTTP 200 — all modules resolve correctly. Let me also check the validate-app hook to see if I need to update it.
The feature doesn't add new routes (it's a component overlay on existing PLP pages), so no validate-app.sh update needed. Let me verify git status and commit.

All 4 implementation files are already committed and tracked. The implementation was done in commit `d012578`. The tests pass (26/26), and the dev server returns HTTP 200. Let me now check the pipeline state and complete it.
Good - the pipeline state shows `storefront-dev` is `pending`. The implementation is already committed. Now I need to mark it complete.

The `storefront-dev` phase for the `product-quick-view` feature is now complete. Here's a summary:  ## Completion Summary  ### Implementation (already committed in `d012578`) All 4 files per the spec were created and are tracked in git:  1. **`overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx`** — ProductTile override that wraps the base tile with a Quick View overlay bar (full-width, semi-transparent, slide-up on hover for desktop, always visible on mobile) 2. **`overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js`** — 12 unit tests covering overlay bar rendering, interaction (click opens modal, preventDefault, stopPropagation), exclusions (sets/bundles/missing ID), prop forwarding, and visual states 3. **`overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx`** — QuickViewModal component using Chakra Modal + `useProductViewModal` hook + `ProductView` with loading spinner, error state, and full product view 4. **`overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js`** — 14 unit tests covering modal shell, content rendering, error state, accessibility (aria-label, Escape key, focus), and ProductView prop forwarding  ### Verification Results - ✅ **Unit tests:** 26/26 passed (100% statement/line coverage) - ✅ **Dev server:** HTTP 200 — all webpack modules resolve correctly - ✅ **SSR safety:** No `window`/`document` access, no hydration mismatch risks - ✅ **Pipeline state:** `storefront-dev` marked as `done`

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && npm start & ↵ SERVER_PID=$! ↵ echo "Server PID: $SERVER_PID" ↵ # Wait for webpack to compile ↵ for i in $(seq 1 24); do ↵ sleep 5 ↵ HTTP_STATUS=$(cu
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view storefront-dev 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark storefront-dev complete for product-quick-view" 2>&1

items.label: storefront-unit-test
messages: Now let me read the existing test files that were created by the implementation phase:
Good - I can see the implementation and test files. Let me run the existing tests to see their status:
All 26 tests pass. Now let me verify coverage and check if the spec requires any additional tests I should add. Let me review the spec's test cases against what's implemented:
All 26 tests pass with 100% statement/line coverage. Let me check the spec for missing test cases and address the uncovered branch on line 28 of product-tile:
Line 28 - the uncovered branch is the `product?.name` fallback (when `productName` is undefined but `name` exists). Let me add the missing spec tests. I'll enhance both test files:
Now let me add the missing spec tests for the QuickViewModal (imageSize="sm" verification):
All 31 tests pass with **100% coverage** across all metrics (statements, branches, functions, lines) for both files. Now let me run the full test suite to ensure no regressions:
There's 1 failed test suite, but it's not showing the failure in the tail output. Let me check which one failed:
The failure is in `e2e/storefront-smoke.spec.ts` — an E2E test file, not a unit test. Let me verify it's a pre-existing issue and not related to my changes:

This is a pre-existing infrastructure issue (Playwright class extends error — the E2E test suite can't even import Playwright in the Jest environment). This is completely unrelated to my unit test changes. All 31 unit tests pass with 100% coverage.  Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The storefront-unit-test phase is complete. Here's a summary:  ## Summary  **Feature:** product-quick-view | **Phase:** storefront-unit-test | **Status:** ✅ Complete  ### Test Results - **31 tests passing** across 2 test suites - **100% code coverage** on all metrics (statements, branches, functions, lines)  ### QuickViewModal Tests (15 tests) | Category | Tests | |---|---| | Modal Shell | Loading spinner, ProductView rendering, data-testid, aria-label, closed state, close button, error state | | Content Integration | Product data passing, "View Full Details" link, Add to Cart button, showFullLink prop, imageSize="sm" prop, isProductLoading | | Accessibility | Escape key closes modal, aria-label fallback for missing product name |  ### ProductTile Override Tests (16 tests) | Category | Tests | |---|---| | Overlay Bar Rendering | Standard product bar, eye icon + text, aria-label, no bar for sets/bundles/missing ID, prop forwarding | | Interaction | Click opens modal, preventDefault, stopPropagation, close hides modal | | Visual States | role="group" container | | Edge Cases | Undefined product, name fallback (`product.name` when `productName` missing), empty aria-label, standard type without ID |  ### Key Improvements Made - Added **5 additional edge case tests** beyond the initial implementation to achieve 100% branch coverage - Added `imageSize="sm"` prop verification test per spec requirement - Enhanced ProductView mock with `data-image-size` attribute for prop assertion - All commerce-sdk-react hooks properly mocked — no live API calls in tests

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view storefront-unit-test 2>&1

items.label: create-draft-pr
messages: Now I have all the information needed. Let me check if there's an open PR already or if I need to create a new one, and then build the PR.
Now let me create the draft PR with a well-structured body:
Draft PR #71 created successfully. Now let me complete the pipeline step.

The **create-draft-pr** phase is complete. Here's a summary:  ## Summary  **Feature:** `product-quick-view` | **Phase:** `create-draft-pr` | **Status:** ✅ Complete  ### What was done - Created **Draft PR #71**: [feat(commerce): add product quick view modal on PLP tiles](https://github.com/RomanKaliupinMelonusa/DAGent-t/pull/71) - PR targets `project/pwa-kit` base branch from `feature/product-quick-view` - Structured PR body includes: Executive Summary, Key Insights, Risk Assessment (LOW ✅), Testing Evidence (31 tests, 100% coverage), Changes table, and Suggested Reviewers  ### PR Highlights | Metric | Value | |---|---| | Implementation files | 2 (ProductTile override + QuickViewModal) | | Test files | 2 (16 + 15 = 31 tests) | | Coverage | 100% all metrics | | Risk level | LOW | | Architecture | PWA Kit override pattern — zero base template modifications |

items.label: e2e-author
messages: Now let me look at the actual implementation files to understand the exact data-testid contracts and component structure.
Now let me check what category URLs are available and understand the app routing:

I have a comprehensive understanding of the feature. Let me now create the E2E test file.  **Data-testid contract map:** - `quick-view-btn` — Quick View overlay bar on product tiles - `quick-view-modal` — Modal content container - `quick-view-spinner` — Loading spinner in modal - `quick-view-error` — Error/unavailable state in modal  **User flows to test:** 1. Quick View button visibility on PLP product tiles 2. Modal opens on click with loading → content transition 3. Modal close via X button, Escape key, overlay click 4. URL preservation (no navigation away from PLP) 5. Accessibility (aria-label on button and modal) 6. Mobile viewport (bar always visible)
Now let me run the self-review gate to check for banned patterns:
No banned patterns found. Let me also verify the TypeScript compiles correctly and the file is well-formed:
All errors are from `node_modules/@types/node` (version mismatch), not from my test file. Let me verify there are no errors in my file specifically:
No errors in my test file. Let me also do a quick syntax check with roam:
Commit successful. Now let me mark the pipeline phase as complete:

Pipeline state is managed by the orchestrator as expected. The e2e-author phase is complete.  ## Summary  **Task completed: e2e-author phase for product-quick-view**  I authored `/workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/e2e/product-quick-view.spec.ts` — a dedicated Playwright E2E test file with **16 tests** across 5 test groups:  ### Test Coverage  | Group | Tests | What's Verified | |---|---|---| | **Overlay Bar on PLP** (4) | Button visibility, text content, aria-label, semantic `<button>` element | | **Modal Lifecycle** (6) | Open via click, loading→content transition, aria-label with product name, close via X button, close via Escape, URL preservation | | **Modal Content** (3) | Product image rendering, Add to Cart button, "View Full Details" link | | **Mobile Viewport** (2) | Bar visible without hover at 375×812, modal opens on tap | | **Edge Cases** (2) | Different products per tile, rapid open-close-open resilience |  ### data-testid Contracts Targeted - `quick-view-btn` — overlay bar button on product tiles - `quick-view-modal` — modal content container   - `quick-view-spinner` — loading spinner in modal - `quick-view-error` — error/unavailable state  ### Compliance - ✅ No `networkidle` or `waitForTimeout` patterns (verified via grep) - ✅ Browser diagnostics capture (console errors + failed requests) - ✅ Screenshots on failure - ✅ `domcontentloaded` for navigation waits - ✅ Explicit locator waits with `waitFor({state: 'visible'})` - ✅ `data-testid` selectors via `page.getByTestId()` as primary locator strategy - ✅ `force: true` on desktop clicks (bar hidden until hover) - ✅ Dedicated feature test file (not appended to smoke tests)
